title: Stack auxiliary next-state tasks with the main PitNextLap model
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop work uses cascaded multi-task heads, combining next-lap stop classification with a remaining-laps-to-stop target.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop work uses cascaded multi-task heads, combining next-lap stop classification with a remaining-laps-to-stop target. The dataset exposes natural auxiliary labels such as next Stint increment, next Compound change, TyreLife reset, and laps-until-next PitStop that can make the pit decision boundary sharper.
implementation_hint: Generate fold-safe OOF predictions for auxiliary tasks: WillStintIncreaseNextLap, WillCompoundChangeNextLap, TyreLifeResetNextLap, and LapsToNextPit bucket. Feed these OOF probabilities/regression outputs into the final AutoGluon/GBM stack, and create test auxiliaries from models unless the shifted-observation identity is validated.
expected_effect: Could improve ranking where PitNextLap is ambiguous but correlated next-state transitions are easier to learn.
risk: Auxiliary labels can leak if built with future rows inside folds; gains depend on clean OOF stacking discipline and enough training time.
